title: Citrusy Comforting Brazilian Mussels Tagine
description: A hearty Brazilian dish featuring mussels, citrus, and a comforting glassy-jelly sauce, all cooked to perfection in a ceramic tagine.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of freshly squeezed orange juice and 1 tablespoon of freshly squeezed lime juice to the tagine.
Add 1 pound of mussels to the tagine, cover, and cook for 3-5 minutes or until the mussels open.
Add 1 cup of fish stock, 1 tablespoon of honey, and 1 teaspoon of grated ginger to the tagine. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and cook for an additional 5-7 minutes or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Season the sauce with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of fresh lime juice.
Serve the mussels in the tagine, spooning some of the sauce over the top.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro, if desired.
